templates.text: Books burning. Not in protest, not in anger — in inevitability. This is what knowledge does when no one holds it anymore.
You saved what you could. The archive does not judge what you could not reach. It simply catalogues what remains.
The smoke carries words. Not legible — combustion does not preserve syntax — but something lingers in the particulate air.
Someone set this fire before you arrived. The dungeon does not explain who or why. Both answers would disturb you differently.
Each question answered was a volume pulled from the flames. Each failure was one you could not reach in time.
Pages curl. Bindings split. A century of accumulation undone in the time it takes to answer four questions.
What you saved will stay saved. What was lost is gone. The distinction between the two is your performance.
Libraries burn more often than they should. The dungeon has watched enough of them to stop being surprised.
The last shelf, still intact. You do not know why the fire spared it. Possibly the fire does not know either.
